data.current_description: Search provider directory by name, NPI, or location.
data.summary_suggestion: Search for healthcare practitioners by criteria
data.description_suggestion: Search the provider directory for FHIR Practitioner resources by name, NPI (National Provider Identifier), location, or other criteria. Accepts multiple search parameters to filter results. Returns a FHIR Bundle containing matching Practitioner resources. Requires authentication via Patient Access Token or Application Access Token.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about searchable parameters, return format, and authentication requirements. Enhanced version clarifies that results are returned as a FHIR Bundle, specifies authentication is required, and explains the search parameter options more comprehensively for better AI agent decision-making about when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Search FHIR resources with filtering, pagination, and includes.
data.description_suggestion: Search FHIR resources by type with optional filtering, pagination, and related resource includes. Accepts search parameters specific to the resource type. Returns a FHIR Bundle containing matching resources and pagination metadata. Supports standard FHIR search syntax including _count for page size, _offset for pagination, and _include for related resources.
data.reasoning: Original description mentions capabilities but lacks clarity on input parameters (search parameters, pagination mechanics) and output format (Bundle structure). Enhanced description specifies parameter types, return format, and common search parameters to improve AI agent understanding of how to construct and interpret search requests.

data.description_suggestion: Generate an International Patient Summary (IPS) document for the specified patient. The IPS is a standardized FHIR document containing essential patient information including demographics, active problems, medications, allergies, and recent clinical encounters. Returns a FHIR Bundle with document composition and referenced resources in IPS-compliant format.
data.reasoning: Original summary uses operation syntax notation which is less intuitive. Description is too brief and doesn't explain what an IPS contains or its format. data.current_description: Create SMART Health Card verifiable credential for a patient.
data.description_suggestion: Create a SMART Health Card verifiable credential for the specified patient. Accepts optional parameters to specify which clinical data to include in the credential (e.g., vaccination records, test results). Returns a SMART Health Card in JWT format containing cryptographically signed patient health information. The credential can be shared with third parties for verification.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks detail about input parameters (what data can be included), output format (JWT), and use case (shareable verification). Enhanced description specifies parameter options, JWT format, and the credential's purpose for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'patientExpunge' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Summary is clear but description lacks critical context about authorization requirements, return format, and irreversibility. Enhanced description emphasizes the permanent nature of the operation, specifies authorization requirements, clarifies the OperationOutcome return, and warns about irreversibility to ensure AI agents understand the severity and proper use of this destructive operation.

data.current_description: Permanently delete all patient compartment data.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently delete all FHIR resources in the patient compartment for the specified patient. This operation irreversibly removes all patient data including clinical records, observations, and related resources. Requires explicit patient authorization. Returns an OperationOutcome indicating success or failure. This action cannot be undone and should only be used when patient data deletion is explicitly requested.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description lacks critical context about authorization requirements, return format, and irreversibility. Enhanced description emphasizes the permanent nature of the operation, specifies authorization requirements, clarifies the OperationOutcome return, and warns about irreversibility to ensure AI agents understand the severity and proper use of this destructive operation.

data.current_description: Extract and parse FHIR data using a ViewDefinition resource.
data.description_suggestion: Execute a ViewDefinition to extract and transform FHIR data according to specified extraction rules. Accepts a ViewDefinition resource (by reference or inline) that defines which FHIR resources and data elements to extract. Returns extracted data in the format specified by the ViewDefinition, typically as structured data or a custom document format. Useful for generating custom reports or data exports from patient records.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description is vague about inputs (ViewDefinition format/reference) and outputs (return format varies). Enhanced description clarifies that ViewDefinition can be provided by reference or inline, explains the variable output format, and provides context about use cases (reports, exports) to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.
